description: A timed-out POST is retried without an idempotency key, duplicating a create or charge that the first attempt already applied.
body: Failure mode: the client retries a non-idempotent POST after a timeout, but the first attempt actually reached the server and applied the effect before the response was lost, so the retry creates a second record or charges twice. Detection signals: duplicate resources/charges correlated with timeouts; the bug appears only under network flakiness; retry logic that does not distinguish idempotent methods. Do not retry mutations without a dedup mechanism. Safer fallback: attach a client-generated idempotency key the server deduplicates, or use an idempotent method/conditional update, and verify with a test that a retried mutation applies exactly once.
